samples.xml_description:
<module id="pulse_detect">
    <description>Implement a module for pulse detection. The 1-bit input signal data_in is a continuous input, which is triggered by clk. When "data_in" changes from 0 to 1 to 0(3 cycles), this is considered as a "pulse". The indicating signal dataout is 1 at the end cycle of the "pulse", and then returns to 0 until the corresponding pulse appears again. For example, if data_in is 01010(5 cycles), the data_out is 00101.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal (active low).</input>
        <input id="data_in">One-bit input signal.</input>
        <output id="data_out">Output signal indicating the presence of pulses.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <state id="IDLE">Initial state.</state>
        <state id="PULSE">State after detecting a pulse.</state>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <register id="state" type="state" init_value="IDLE">Current state of the pulse detection process.</register>
        <wire id="next_state">Next state of the pulse detection process.</wire>
        <always_comb>
            <case>
                <item test="~rst_n">
                    <assign next_state = IDLE;</assign>
                </item>
                <item test="state == PULSE && data_in == 0">
                    <assign next_state = IDLE;</assign>
                </item>
                <default>
                    <assign next_state = state + 1;</assign>
                </default>
            </case>
        </always_comb>
        <always_ff posedge clk negedge rst_n>
            <if cond="~rst_n">
                <assign state <= IDLE;
                <assign data_out <= 0;
            </if>
            <else>
                <assign state <= next_state;
                <assign data_out <= (state == PULSE && data_in == 0);
            </else>
        </always_ff>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>Implement the logic for pulse detection and output generation based on the provided description.</implementation>
</module>
